# gandi_livedns_record (Resource)

Manages a single LiveDNS rrset (record) on a Gandi domain. The domain must use Gandi LiveDNS nameservers for records to resolve.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "gandi_livedns_record" "www" {
  domain = "example.com"
  name   = "www"
  type   = "A"
  ttl    = 3600
  values = ["203.0.113.10"]
}

# TXT values must be quoted, as Gandi stores them wrapped in double quotes.
resource "gandi_livedns_record" "spf" {
  domain = "example.com"
  name   = "@"
  type   = "TXT"
  ttl    = 10800
  values = ["\"v=spf1 include:_mailcust.gandi.net ~all\""]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain` (String) The domain (FQDN) the record belongs to.
- `name` (String) Record name relative to the domain, e.g. `www` or `@` for the apex.
- `type` (String) Record type, e.g. `A`, `AAAA`, `CNAME`, `MX`, `TXT`.
- `values` (Set of String) Record values. For CNAME/MX/NS use fully-qualified names ending with a dot.

### Optional

- `ttl` (Number) Time to live in seconds (300–2592000). Defaults to 10800.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) `<domain>/<name>/<type>`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# LiveDNS records are imported as <domain>/<name>/<type>.
terraform import gandi_livedns_record.www example.com/www/A
```
